title: Research
text:
Ocean Glider Lab


Research


Autonomous observing, coastal processes, and data products that support long-term study of the Oregon shelf and beyond.





Autonomous observing


Glider deployments, sensor systems, and repeat transects that capture changing coastal conditions over time.




Coastal processes


Currents, upwelling, oxygen variability, and the physical drivers that connect the shelf to marine ecosystems.




Data products


Analysis, visualization, and well-documented datasets that support students, collaborators, and field teams.

title: Jace Marquardt
text:
Ocean Glider Lab


Jace Marquardt


Autonomous underwater glider pilot and technician for the Oregon State University Glider Research Group.

title: Education
text:
Ocean Glider Lab


Education


Academic preparation and continuing learning across oceanography, field work, and scientific computing.





Graduate training in oceanography, marine science, or a related coastal observing field.


Undergraduate foundation in the physical sciences, mathematics, engineering, or environmental studies.


Ongoing learning through field deployments, coding, mentoring, and collaboration.

title: About Me
text:
Ocean Glider Lab


About Me


A brief profile, research focus, and a few quick facts about my work with coastal observing systems.



Ocean Glider Lab studies how coastal circulation, water properties, and climate variability shape ocean conditions along the shelf. My work combines autonomous underwater vehicles, gliders, ship-based measurements, and data products that make long observing records easier to use.



FocusCoastal oceanography


ToolsGliders, sensors, maps


ApproachField data and analysis


AudienceStudents, collaborators, coastal users

title: Experience
text:
Ocean Glider Lab


Experience


Research, field work, teaching support, and project coordination across coastal observing campaigns.





Research and field work on underwater gliders, moorings, and coastal observing projects.


Lab and teaching support for students working with ocean data, instrumentation, and scientific communication.


Project coordination across observing campaigns, data management, and collaborative research efforts.
